chunk_summary: Guidelines for creating executable discharge feeding plans for preterm/LBW/ELBW infants, including feeding behaviors, caregiver tools, and follow-up protocols to ensure growth and safety post-discharge.
learning_objectives: Understand the components of an executable discharge feeding plan for preterm/LBW/ELBW infants.
Learn caregiver monitoring tools, including feeding logs and red flag identification.
Recognize follow-up timelines and criteria for high-risk infants post-discharge.
key_facts: Discharge feeding plans must include primary feed type, measurable supplement, stop/step-down criteria, and a return plan.
India’s SNCU follow-up guidance recommends a home visit within 24 hours of discharge and early repeat contacts in the first weeks.
Tier 3 infants require clinician review within 24–72 hours post-discharge, while Tier 2 infants need review within 1 week.
Caregiver tools include a 3-day feeding log tracking feed times, volumes, top-ups, and signs of feeding-related issues.
Red flags requiring same-day review include poor feeding with lethargy, persistent vomiting, respiratory distress, or failure to meet growth targets.
reference_quote: Government of India SNCU follow-up guidance treats the day of discharge as day one and recommends a home visit within 24 hours, with early repeat contacts during the first weeks.
